name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 2|Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nagara, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{jalink|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{jalink|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885}} or {{jalink|Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{jalink|dōtaku|Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{jalink|5th century|Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' entry for {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} 9 ({{jalink|680|Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Katsuragi District|Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{jalink|Shōwa|Q1324807}} 1 ({{jalink|1312|Q5734}}) of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{jalink|Kakitsu|Q1193238}} 1 ({{jalink|1441|Q6402}}) or {{jalink|Chōkyō|Q968843}} 3 ({{jalink|1489|Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 2|Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nagara, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{jalink|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{jalink|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885}} or {{jalink|Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{jalink|dōtaku|Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{jalink|5th century|Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' entry for {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} 9 ({{jalink|680|Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Katsuragi District|Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{jalink|Shōwa|Q1324807}} 1 ({{jalink|1312|Q5734}}) of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{jalink|Kakitsu|Q1193238}} 1 ({{jalink|1441|Q6402}}) or {{jalink|Chōkyō|Q968843}} 3 ({{jalink|1489|Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 2|Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nagara, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{jalink|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{jalink|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885}} or {{jalink|Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{jalink|dōtaku|Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{jalink|5th century|Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' entry for {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} 9 ({{jalink|680|Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Katsuragi District|Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{jalink|Shōwa|Q1324807}} 1 ({{jalink|1312|Q5734}}) of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{jalink|Kakitsu|Q1193238}} 1 ({{jalink|1441|Q6402}}) or {{jalink|Chōkyō|Q968843}} 3 ({{jalink|1489|Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 2|Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nagara, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{jalink|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{jalink|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885}} or {{jalink|Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{jalink|dōtaku|Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{jalink|5th century|Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' entry for {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} 9 ({{jalink|680|Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Katsuragi District|Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{jalink|Shōwa|Q1324807}} 1 ({{jalink|1312|Q5734}}) of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{jalink|Kakitsu|Q1193238}} 1 ({{jalink|1441|Q6402}}) or {{jalink|Chōkyō|Q968843}} 3 ({{jalink|1489|Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 2|Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nagara, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{jalink|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{jalink|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885}} or {{jalink|Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{jalink|dōtaku|Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{jalink|5th century|Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' entry for {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} 9 ({{jalink|680|Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Katsuragi District|Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{jalink|Shōwa|Q1324807}} 1 ({{jalink|1312|Q5734}}) of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{jalink|Kakitsu|Q1193238}} 1 ({{jalink|1441|Q6402}}) or {{jalink|Chōkyō|Q968843}} 3 ({{jalink|1489|Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 2|Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nagara, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{jalink|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{jalink|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885}} or {{jalink|Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{jalink|dōtaku|Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{jalink|5th century|Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' entry for {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} 9 ({{jalink|680|Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Katsuragi District|Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{jalink|Shōwa|Q1324807}} 1 ({{jalink|1312|Q5734}}) of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{jalink|Kakitsu|Q1193238}} 1 ({{jalink|1441|Q6402}}) or {{jalink|Chōkyō|Q968843}} 3 ({{jalink|1489|Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 2|Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nagara, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{jalink|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{jalink|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885}} or {{jalink|Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{jalink|dōtaku|Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{jalink|5th century|Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}' entry for {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} 9 ({{jalink|680|Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Katsuragi District|Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{jalink|Shōwa|Q1324807}} 1 ({{jalink|1312|Q5734}}) of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{jalink|Kakitsu|Q1193238}} 1 ({{jalink|1441|Q6402}}) or {{jalink|Chōkyō|Q968843}} 3 ({{jalink|1489|Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nagara, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955|WD=Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885|WD=Q11620885}} or {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{ill|lt=dōtaku|Q2312868|WD=Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 9 ({{ill|lt=680|Q23453|WD=Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Katsuragi District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q1324807|WD=Q1324807}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1312|Q5734|WD=Q5734}}) of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{ill|lt=Kakitsu|Q1193238|WD=Q1193238}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1441|Q6402|WD=Q6402}}) or {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|WD=Q968843}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1489|Q6665|WD=Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nagara, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955|WD=Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885|WD=Q11620885}} or {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{ill|lt=dōtaku|Q2312868|WD=Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 9 ({{ill|lt=680|Q23453|WD=Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Katsuragi District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q1324807|WD=Q1324807}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1312|Q5734|WD=Q5734}}) of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{ill|lt=Kakitsu|Q1193238|WD=Q1193238}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1441|Q6402|WD=Q6402}}) or {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|WD=Q968843}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1489|Q6665|WD=Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nagara, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}; its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955|WD=Q11620955}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885|WD=Q11620885}} or {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{ill|lt=dōtaku|Q2312868|WD=Q2312868}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 9 ({{ill|lt=680|Q23453|WD=Q23453}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Katsuragi District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q1324807|WD=Q1324807}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1312|Q5734|WD=Q5734}}) of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{ill|lt=Kakitsu|Q1193238|WD=Q1193238}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1441|Q6402|WD=Q6402}}) or {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|WD=Q968843}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1489|Q6665|WD=Q6665}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なからしんしや こせ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
