== name in kana (P1814) == * ながらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1150005006448 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市名柄271', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長柄神社 (御所市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|qid=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|qid=Q2932|1=October 2}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nagara, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|qid=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}; its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955|qid=Q11620955|1=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885|qid=Q11620885|1=Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine}} or {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|qid=Q11673295|1=Takakamo Shrine}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{ill|lt=dōtaku|Q2312868|qid=Q2312868|1=Dōtaku}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|qid=Q8095|1=5th century}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 9 ({{ill|lt=680|Q23453|qid=Q23453|1=680}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Katsuragi District|Q11620858|qid=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q1324807|qid=Q1324807|1=Shōwa (1312–1317)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1312|Q5734|qid=Q5734|1=1312}}) of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{ill|lt=Kakitsu|Q1193238|qid=Q1193238|1=Kakitsu}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1441|Q6402|qid=Q6402|1=1441}}) or {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|qid=Q968843|1=Chōkyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1489|Q6665|qid=Q6665|1=1489}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]

link|Q22119789}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|qid=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}271 Nagara |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|26|22.82|N|135|43|1.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagara Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before enthronement of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}) |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = Hime-no-Miya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|qid=Q2932|1=October 2}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Nagara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagara Shrine''' (ながらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nagara, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|qid=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}; its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called "Hime-no-Miya." === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one deityPrecinct information board.: * {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (したてるひめのみこと) Texts also present other theories: {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} or Takateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The Kotoshironushi theory derives from '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}' entry "Yamato Province divine lineage, Nagara no Obito" which states the ancestor as "Ame-no-yae Kotoshironushi." The Takateruhime theory derives from {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|Q11620955|qid=Q11620955|1=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} (also popularly called "Nagarahime Shrine") enshrining Takateruhime as associated deity{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Another view says the Nagara clan originally enshrined their ancestor deity, but later traditions linking this shrine as a "hime-no-miya" of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|Q11620885|qid=Q11620885|1=Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine}} or {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|qid=Q11673295|1=Takakamo Shrine}} gave rise to Takateruhime (sister deity of Kotoshironushi) and Shitateruhime (sister deity of {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}) theories{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. === History === Founding is unknown{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}' mentions "Katsuragi Nagae"; "Nagae" and "Nagara" are said to be synonymous, meaning a long ridge of Mount Katsuragi. There is also a theory identifying this place with "Hegumi Nagara Oka-saki" in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' pre-enthronement chronicle of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, year Kimi, 2nd month, 20th day{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}} (another theory identifies it with Nagara-chō in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} by context). Near the shrine are known finds including a {{ill|lt=dōtaku|Q2312868|qid=Q2312868|1=Dōtaku}} (Nagara dōtaku), multi-knob fine-line mirror, and a mid-{{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|qid=Q8095|1=5th century}} chieftain residence site (Nagara site). Texts also note that the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 9 ({{ill|lt=680|Q23453|qid=Q23453|1=680}}), 9th month, 9th day says the emperor held mounted archery at "Nagara no Mori," inferred as this shrine grove{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Katsuragi District|Q11620858|qid=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}}, Yamato Province, as "Nagara Shrine, small, hoe and quiver," indicating listing as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and that hoes and quivers were offered by the court at {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}. Later history is unclear, but ridge tags survive back to {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q1324807|qid=Q1324807|1=Shōwa (1312–1317)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1312|Q5734|qid=Q5734|1=1312}}) of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} for construction/reconstruction of the honden. Also, a 1418 ({{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 25) "Handa-shō Chūshinbun" in the 'Kasuga Shrine documents' records "Nagara no Miya," identified with this shrine{{SFn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === Precinct === [[File:Nagara-jinja (Gose) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden (Nara Prefecture designated cultural property)}}]] The honden is ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}. Exterior is vermilion-lacquered; the roof was formerly {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but is now copper-plate roofed. Zen-style details are seen; though construction date is unknown, it is estimated to date back to the mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. Seventeen ridge tags survive, oldest from 1312; from style they are inferred to correspond to tags of {{ill|lt=Kakitsu|Q1193238|qid=Q1193238|1=Kakitsu}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1441|Q6402|qid=Q6402|1=1441}}) or {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|qid=Q968843|1=Chōkyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1489|Q6665|qid=Q6665|1=1489}}). This honden is designated Nara Prefecture cultural property{{Sfn|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 8|1998年|pp=893-894}}. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden (with 17 ridge tags) (architecture) - designated March 20, Shōwa 33[http://archaeology.ec-net.jp/f-3.html Cultural properties of Gose > Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties] (Gose City Board of Education Cultural Properties Section). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * Local government histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 5 Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=|title=Nara Prefectural History 8 Architecture|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=4626014623|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 8|1998年}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Nagara Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed.|author=Kimura Yoshiichi|year=1985|chapter=Nagara Shrine|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Places - 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|長柄神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === External links === * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/nagara/nagara.html Nagara Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%83%8A%E8%A1%8C/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nagara Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagara Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]
